purpose: Run World Tree style interactive fiction through an OpenAI-compatible LLM without depending on Hermes skill execution.
runtimeLoop: Classify the user turn as slash command, narrative action, setup, card interaction, or out-of-game note.
Apply active module rules and current world model.
Inject matched worldbook entries and imported card context.
Run rule feasibility and continuity checks silently.
Generate immersive narration or command result.
Emit proposed patches or command plans for persistent changes.

outputContract.narrative: Primary immersive prose, written as the DM or active character mode requires.
outputContract.choices: Optional short action suggestions when helpful.
outputContract.engineNotes: Brief non-spoiler notes only when a command, patch, rule block, or state proposal matters.
outputContract.patch: JSON-like proposed patch only for persistence requests.

modules.M1: World/container isolation and character ownership guard.
modules.M2: Worldbook keyword/semantic entry trigger and injection.
modules.M3: Dynamic world state variables and event consequences.
modules.M4_M7_M10: Organizations, hierarchy, relationships, key figures, species dimensions.
modules.M8: Character presets and character state snapshots.
modules.M9: Character cognition, secrets, and knowledge boundaries.
modules.M11: Scene session, scene transitions, nested scenes, scene summaries.
modules.M13: Five-layer narration: character, environment, plot, tone, memory.
modules.M15: World rules, feasibility, costs, physics or magic constraints.
modules.M15c: Narrative quality audit: consistency, pacing, style, scene continuity.
modules.M17: Random event framework with weighted proposals.
modules.M18: Scene direction prediction and narrative inertia.
modules.M19: Character-card driven mode: character speaks directly, DM retreats, worldbook proposal mode is reduced.
modules.CREATION: Worldbook creation toolkit; used for setup and expansion, not normal runtime.
